title: Microwave Apricot Jam
excerpt: It's apricot season now, and putting your own jam on the table is such a treat. With the microwave you can cook jam in a jiffy. This is the first time I've tried it, and it's the prettiest jam I've ever made. Make sure your apricots aren't …
html: It's apricot season now, and putting your own jam on the table is such a treat.  With the microwave you can cook jam in a jiffy.  This is the first time I've tried it, and it's the prettiest jam I've ever made. Make sure your apricots aren't too ripe. Make sure you use a nice deep bowl to work in, because the jam boils up. If not, do it in two batches or halve the recipe.

steps: Wash the apricots. Halve the apricots and remove the pips. Keep about 5 pips aside.
Layer the apricots, water, lemon juice and sugar in a microwave-safe bowl. Press the pips in between.
Microwave on 100% for 10 minutes. Take out of the microwave and stir the sugar through.
Now set your microwave to 35 minutes and put the bowl back in. You're still working on 100% power.
Now microwave the apricots and stir every 5 minutes. Be careful, it's extremely hot. Use an oven mitt.

To test whether your jam is ready you can measure it with a thermometer, and if the temperature is 220F or 105C, you know it's ready. If you don't have a thermometer you can put a saucer in the freezer. Spoon a bit of jam onto the cold saucer and draw your finger through the jam. If the line doesn't close up immediately, your jam is ready.
Remove all the pips from the jam.
Spoon the jam into sterilised jars.
